agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to replace 1965 Zoning Bylaw with new 2026 version
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to repeal Burnaby's current Zoning Bylaw (from 1965) and replace it with a new Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026. This proposed bylaw would also introduce new Development Permit Area Guidelines and reassign some properties to different zoning districts. If approved, this would change the rules for what you can build and do on your property. The decision has not been made yet — this is up for discussion and a vote at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to speak up about how the new rules would affect your property or business.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
BURNABY ZONING BYLAW - PROPOSED REPEAL AND REPLACEMENT
Purpose: To seek Council approval to repeal Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, including new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignment.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Housing Authority Year 2 operating funding request
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation's request for Year 2 operating funding. This is a proposal to provide ongoing financial support to the housing authority for its second year of operations. The decision has not been made yet — council will vote on whether to approve the funding at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2
BHA - ASSISTANCE REQUEST FOR YEAR 2 OPERATING FUNDING
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation’s assistance request for Year 2 Operating Funding.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on 2026 property tax rates for Burnaby businesses
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on proposed property tax rates for 2026. This includes the municipal portion (city services), the regional portion (services shared across the Metro Vancouver region), and special rates for businesses located in a Business Improvement Area (BIA). If approved, these rates will determine how much property tax you pay next year. The decision has not been made yet — Council will discuss and vote on the proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — your 2026 property tax bill depends on the rates approved.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Food Service
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3
2026 PROPERTY TAX RATES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for 2026 municipal, regional and business improvement area (BIA) property tax rates.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to appoint an Approving Officer
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to appoint an Approving Officer. An Approving Officer is a person who has the authority to approve plans for new subdivisions (dividing land into smaller lots) and road closures. This is a routine administrative appointment, but it affects how land development applications are processed in Burnaby. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider and vote on the appointment at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from you at this time. Watch for the council decision if you are involved in land development or subdivision applications.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4
APPOINTMENT OF APPROVING OFFICER
Purpose: To seek Council approval to appoint an Approving Officer.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on retaining wall height variance at 8012 11th Ave
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a Development Variance Permit (DVP) for a property at 8012 11th Avenue. A DVP is a special permission that lets a property owner break certain rules in the zoning bylaw — in this case, the rules about how tall retaining walls can be. The owner wants to build retaining walls that are taller than what the current bylaw allows. Council will decide whether to approve this variance at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: If you own or develop property near 8012 11th Avenue, watch for the council decision — it could set a precedent for similar height variances in your area.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5
DVP #26-01 - 8012 11TH AVENUE - RETAINING WALLS HEIGHT VARIANCES
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Development Variance Permit (DVP) #26-01.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on insurance contract renewals
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on awarding contracts for the city's annual property and liability insurance programs. This is a routine renewal of insurance coverage that protects city assets and operations. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming meeting and has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.6
2026 INSURANCE PROGRAMS - CONTRACT RENEWALS
Purpose: To request approval to award insurance contracts for the annual property and liability insurance programs.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to approve Heritage Commission trip to 2026 BC conference
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to allow members of Burnaby's Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC, from May 4-6, 2026. The CHC advises Council on heritage matters, and this trip would help members learn and network. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider approving the travel and related costs at an upcoming meeting.

key_dates.label: Heritage BC Conference in Squamish takes place

agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.7
2026 HERITAGE BC CONFERENCE
Purpose: To seek Council approval for members of the Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC from May 4-6, 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract award for networking software licenses
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses. If approved, this would authorize the city to purchase the software needed to run its computer networks. This is a routine technology purchase that keeps city systems running, but it does not directly create new rules or fees for local businesses.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.8
CA - NETWORKING SOFTWARE LICENSES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for new civic facility furniture
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply, delivery, and installation of office and facility furniture for a new civic facility. If approved, this contract would cover all the furniture and equipment needed to fit out the new building. The decision is on the agenda for the upcoming council meeting and has not yet been finalized.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.9
CA - NEW CIVIC FACILITY FIT-OUT, FURNISHINGS AND EQUIPMENT
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply, delivery and installation of office and facility furniture.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract change for external audit services
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change for the city's external audit services. This means council will decide whether to approve a change to the agreement with the company that audits the city's financial records. If approved, the change would alter the terms of the existing contract. The decision is not final yet — council will discuss and vote on it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.10
CC - EXTERNAL AUDIT SERVICES
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for External Audit Services.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Contract change for geotechnical work at Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change (a formal amendment to an existing agreement) for geotechnical consulting services at the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex. Geotechnical consultants study soil and ground conditions to ensure buildings are safe and stable. If approved, this change would adjust the scope or cost of the current consulting contract for this recreation complex project.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from you at this time. Watch for the council decision — this could affect the project timeline or costs.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.11
CC - BURNABY LAKE RECREATION COMPLEX GEOTECHNICAL CONSULTING SERVICES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for geotechnical consultant services for the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex project.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on Spring 2026 community grant recipients
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving the recommended recipients for the Spring 2026 community grant program. This is a proposed decision — if approved, the grants would provide funding to local community groups and non-profits. The vote will happen at an upcoming council meeting. Business owners who partner with or rely on community organizations should watch for the outcome, as it could affect local programs and services.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1
ECC - SPRING 2026 COMMUNITY GRANT APPLICATIONS
Purpose: To seek Council approval for the recommended grant recipients for the Spring 2026 intake.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Mid-year 2026 fee changes to Burnaby Fees and Charges Bylaw
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider mid-year 2026 amendments to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. This proposed update would change certain fees listed in the bylaw — the city's official list of charges for permits, licences, and services. The decision has not been made yet; council is set to vote on the proposed fee changes at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and review the proposed fee changes to see if they affect your business licences or permits.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.2
FMC - BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND CHARGES BYLAW - 2026 MID-YEAR AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To propose mid-year 2026 fee amendments to certain fee schedules within the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed Tenant Protection Bylaw and a supporting Tenant Protection Framework. If approved, this new bylaw (a local law) would create new rules to protect renters, such as limits on certain evictions and requirements for landlords. Council will consider endorsing the framework and bylaw at an upcoming meeting. This is not yet law — it is a proposal under discussion.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — if passed, this bylaw could affect how you manage rental properties in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.3
PDC - PROPOSED TENANT PROTECTION FRAMEWORK AND TENANT PROTECTION BYLAW AND ASSOCIATED BYLAW AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement of the proposed tenant protection framework and Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw and associated bylaw amendments.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on Southeast Bikeway upgrades
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on proposed improvements for the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project. If approved, this project would add new bike lanes, crossings, and safety features along the route. The decision is not final yet — Council will discuss and vote on the plan at an upcoming meeting. Business owners near the bikeway should watch for the outcome, as construction could affect traffic, parking, and customer access.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — if approved, prepare for possible construction impacts on your business access and parking.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.4
TC - SOUTHEAST BIKEWAY UPGRADE PROJECT - PROPOSED IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the proposed improvements as part of the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update design rules for safer intersections
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to update the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards. The change would require new 'protected intersection' designs that make crossings safer for people walking and biking. This is not yet approved — it is up for discussion and a vote at an upcoming council meeting. If passed, the new rules would apply to future road and development projects in the town centre area.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and consider attending or submitting comments if your business or property is in the Burnaby Town Centre.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.5
TC - PROPOSED AMENDMENT TO THE BURNABY TOWN CENTRE DESIGN STANDARDS TO INCORPORATE PROTECTED INTERSECTION GEOMETRY
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement to amend the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards to incorporate protected intersection geometry, improve road safety for pedestrians and cyclists and support all-ages-and-abilities mobility.

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby rejects Brentwood Park SSMUH exemption; Building Bylaw change considered
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will receive a staff report recommending against exempting Brentwood Park from provincial Small-Scale Multi-Unit Housing (SSMUH) rules. Instead, staff propose amending the City-wide Building Bylaw to require compliance with Restrictive Covenants and Statutory Building Schemes as part of Building Permit approval. This would apply to all properties with such covenants city-wide, including residential, commercial, and industrial, and could limit suites and duplexes while increasing application costs and processing times.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Monitor the April 28, 2026 Council meeting for the decision on the Building Bylaw amendment, which could affect development options on any Burnaby property with a Restrictive Covenant or Statutory Building Scheme.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting on Building Bylaw amendment for Restrictive Covenants

agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.1
BRENTWOOD PARK SSMUH MOTION - RESTRICTIVE COVENANTS AND STATUTORY BUILDING SCHEMES
Purpose: To respond to Council’s September 9, 2025, motion regarding exempting Brentwood Park properties with Restrictive Covenants from Provincial SSMUH requirements.

attachments.title: Brentwood Park SSMUH Motion - Restrictive Covenants and Statutory Building Schem

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive March 2026 building permit activity report
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a report on building permits issued in Burnaby from March 1 to March 31, 2026. This report is for information only — it shows how much construction activity happened in the city during that month. No new rules or fees are being proposed. The report helps Council track development trends.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.2
SU: BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 03 FROM MARCH 1 - MARCH 31, 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction activity as reflected by the building permits that have been issued for the subject period.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Heritage designation for 3789 Royal Oak Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider an 'as-found report' and 'Statement of Significance' for the property at 3789 Royal Oak Avenue. A Statement of Significance is a formal document that explains why a building or site is historically important. This is the first step toward possibly designating the property as a municipal heritage resource. If designated, future changes to the building's exterior or structure would need special permission from the city. Council has not yet voted on this — it is being presented for information and discussion at this stage.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the next council meeting — if you own or plan to buy this property, attend or submit comments before any heritage designation vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.3
CHC - AS-FOUND REPORT AND STATEMENT OF SIGNIFICANCE FOR HERITAGE RESOURCES AT 3789 ROYAL OAK AVENUE
Purpose: To provide Council an as-found report and Statement of Significance for heritage resources located at 7389 Royal Oak Avenue for information.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby solid waste and recycling 2025 annual report
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider the City of Burnaby's 2025 annual report on solid waste and recycling collection. This report summarizes how the city's garbage, recycling, and organics collection system performed last year. It is a review of current operations, not a proposed new bylaw or fee change. The report is up for discussion at the next council meeting.
agenda_briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.4
EC - SOLID WASTE AND RECYCLING (SWR) 2025 ANNUAL REPORT
Purpose: To provide Council with a summary of the City of Burnaby solid waste and recycling collection system performance.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider operational update on food sustainability measures
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will receive an operational update on food sustainability measures in the city. This is a report to Council, not a final decision. The update will cover current programs and actions the city is taking to improve food sustainability. Council will consider the information and may give direction to staff on next steps. No new rules or bylaws are being voted on at this time.
agenda_briefs.industries: Food Service, Grocery & Food Retail, Agriculture
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.5
EC - SU: ENHANCING FOOD SUSTAINABILITY IN BURNABY
Purpose: To provide Council with an operational update on food sustainability measures within the City of Burnaby.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Safety upgrades at Central Boulevard and Metropolis parkade intersection
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and the Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access. The proposal includes changes to traffic signals, crosswalks, and signage to make the area safer for drivers, pedestrians, and cyclists. A final decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.6
TC - CENTRAL BOULEVARD AND PARKADE INERSECTION SAFETY IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To provide Council with an overview of the proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on rezoning for hotel, more homes at 3900 Grand Promenade
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning amendment for four properties near the Carrigan site. The developer wants to swap some planned office space for a hotel and restaurants, and add 362 more market strata (condo) units. This would make Tower 1B seven storeys taller and Tower 1A four storeys taller than previously approved. The developer also proposes to meet the city's inclusionary (affordable housing) requirement for Phase 1 only, by transferring that obligation to the Carrigan site instead of building it on these lands. This is a change from the rezoning already approved in September 2024.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit a written comment to Burnaby City Council before the vote.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14808 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 7, 2026 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 6950 Austin Rd., 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place)
Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two market strata residential buildings of 80 (Tower 1B) and 73 (Tower 1A) storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. As part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to replace a portion of the commercial office use with hotel and restaurant uses, and add 362 additional market strata units from the development concept approved under REZ #18-32, resulting in an additional seven storeys for Tower 1B and four storeys for Tower 1A. In addition, as part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to provide the inclusionary requirement associated with Phase 1 only, to be transferred to the Carrigan site, which is an amendment from the previous rezoning approval.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 12-storey mixed-use development at 6535 Kingsway
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw amendment that would allow a 12-storey mixed-use building at 6535 Kingsway. The project includes 290 residential units, ground-floor commercial space facing Kingsway, and underground parking. This proposal is consistent with the city's Official Community Plan and the Edmonds Community Plan. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — you may want to attend or submit comments if this development affects your property or business.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14809 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 8, 2026 - REZ #25-09 (6535 Kingsway)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a 12-storey mixed-use multiple-family development with a double-height commercial storey at street level comprised of 290 residential units, ground-oriented commercial uses fronting Kingsway, and underground parking. The proposal is consistent with the applicable OCP and the Edmonds Community Plan designations for the site.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Five-storey self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw amendment that would allow a five-storey self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue. The project follows the city's 1998 Official Community Plan (the long-term vision for land use) and approved transition rules. If approved, the rezoning would permit construction of an enclosed storage facility on the site.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit written comments to Burnaby City Council before the vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14810 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 9, 2026 - REZ #25-21 (2757 Norland Avenue)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage building. The proposal is being advanced in accordance with the applicable 1998 OCP policies and approved transitionary provisions.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot bylaw amendment
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw that would create a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, this would change the rules for where and how these devices can be used on city streets. The decision has not been made yet — this is an upcoming proposal on the council agenda.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this pilot could affect how your business operates if you use or sell e-bikes or e-scooters.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot in parks
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would allow e-bikes and electric kick scooters in certain Burnaby parks as part of a pilot program. The amendment to the Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 would create temporary rules for where and how these devices can be used. This is not yet law — council will decide at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this pilot could affect where you can ride e-bikes or e-scooters in Burnaby parks.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment for e-bike and e-scooter pilot enforcement
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update how the city enforces rules for e-bikes and electric kick scooters as part of a pilot program. The amendment to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 would allow the city to issue tickets and fines for violations related to these devices. This is not yet law — it is up for approval at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, new fines for e-bike and e-scooter violations could affect your business operations.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026 (e-bike and electric kick scooter programs)
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026 sets property tax rates
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would establish the 2026 property tax rates for all taxable land and buildings in Burnaby. This bylaw, called the Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026, is required under sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter (the main law that gives cities their powers). If approved, it will set how much property tax you will pay next year based on your property's assessed value. The decision has not been made yet — this is an upcoming item on the council agenda.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this will directly affect your 2026 property tax bill.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.4
#14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter
